headline: Salesforce, Other Software Stocks Are Scraping Themselves Off the Floor
summary: Now software stocks are scraping themselves off the floor—but are still behind the curve as big investors stick with what has worked.  Jefferies software analyst Brent Thill calls the rebound a correction from an overblown selloff.  While semi stocks rode the wave of the AI data center investment boom last year, sentiment soured on enterprise software makers like Atlassian,  Workday  and Salesforce.

raw.headline: Salesforce, Other Software Stocks Are Scraping Themselves Off the Floor
raw.summary: Now software stocks are scraping themselves off the floor—but are still behind the curve as big investors stick with what has worked.  Jefferies software analyst Brent Thill calls the rebound a correction from an overblown selloff.  While semi stocks rode the wave of the AI data center investment boom last year, sentiment soured on enterprise software makers like Atlassian,  Workday  and Salesforce.

headline: Workday shares jump as AI demand eases investor concerns
summary: STORY: :: ArchiveShares of Workday jumped as much as 10% Friday morning after the enterprise software maker beat first-quarter sales and profit estimates.The performance eased concerns that AI rivals such as Anthropic could rapidly disrupt demand for traditional vendors.:: WorkdayCompany executives said subscription revenue rose 14%, with new business accounting for 40% of that.Workday also reiterated its annual subscription revenue forecast.It has been adding AI features across its platform to remain competitive, including the March launch of Sana, its conversational AI layer.:: WorkdayThe company's AI-driven tools help organizations automate tasks such as screening job applicants, scheduling interviews and streamlining workforce planning.At least seven brokerages raised their price targets on Workday stock, while two trimmed it after the results.:: ArchiveAnalysts at Barclays, in a note to clients, called the results comforting but not game-changing.While analysts at Jeffries said, quote, "We believe Workday is relatively insulated from AI disruption due to its 80 million users, strong retention, and status as a system of record."

raw.headline: Workday shares jump as AI demand eases investor concerns
raw.summary: STORY: :: ArchiveShares of Workday jumped as much as 10% Friday morning after the enterprise software maker beat first-quarter sales and profit estimates.The performance eased concerns that AI rivals such as Anthropic could rapidly disrupt demand for traditional vendors.:: WorkdayCompany executives said subscription revenue rose 14%, with new business accounting for 40% of that.Workday also reiterated its annual subscription revenue forecast.It has been adding AI features across its platform to remain competitive, including the March launch of Sana, its conversational AI layer.:: WorkdayThe company's AI-driven tools help organizations automate tasks such as screening job applicants, scheduling interviews and streamlining workforce planning.At least seven brokerages raised their price targets on Workday stock, while two trimmed it after the results.:: ArchiveAnalysts at Barclays, in a note to clients, called the results comforting but not game-changing.While analysts at Jeffries said, quote, "We believe Workday is relatively insulated from AI disruption due to its 80 million users, strong retention, and status as a system of record."

headline: Wall Street rises as pressure eases from the bond market
summary: The U.S. stock market is rising toward the finish of an eighth straight winning week on Friday, which would be its longest such streak since 2023.  Ross Stores climbed 7.7% after the off-price retailer reported profit and revenue for the latest quarter that easily cleared analysts’ expectations.  CEO Jim Conroy said it saw strong customer traffic through the three months, and the company may have benefited from households spending their tax refunds.

Ross Stores climbed 7.7% after the off-price retailer reported profit and revenue for the latest quarter that easily cleared analysts’ expectations.  CEO Jim Conroy said it saw strong customer traffic through the three months, and the company may have benefited from households spending their tax refunds.
